CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-21-2015 Continued To Oct-21-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-28-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. AGUSTIN R. PINA OF THE SCHINNER & SHAIN LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-766-9897 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT;
ATTY. SEAN B. JANZEN OF THE LAW OFFICES OF THOMAS F, NOWLAND, TEL. NO. 1-949-221-0005 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT RELEVAD CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT OF QIMEDIA, INC. is overruled in its entirety. Causes of action one and two sufficiently allege a breach of contract claim. "Although breach of the implied covenant often is pleaded as a separate count, a breach of the implied covenant is necessarily a breach of contract." Digerati Holdings, LLC v. Young Money Entertainment, LLC (2011) 194 Cal.App.4th 873, 885. Cause of action three sufficiently alleges concealment based upon Relevad's active concealment and/or exclusive knowledge. See Limandri v. Judkins (1997) 52 Cal.App.4th 326, 336-337. Cause of action four alleges the intentional misrepresentation cause of action with sufficient particularity. Q1Media alleges that Relevad misrepresented that the bot clicks were human clicks from 3/14 - 11/14. (Pars. 55-56 of the Cross-Complaint). Those allegations are sufficiently particular and less specificity is required where the cross-defendant "necessarily possesses full information concerning the facts of the controversy." Committee on Children's Television, Inc. v. General Foods Corp. (1983) 35 Cal.3d 197, 216-217. Cause of action 8 alleges the violation of the UCL claim with reasonable particularity. Order signed in open court. Judge: Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/sb

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-28-2015 Continued To Aug-28-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT RELEVAD CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is continued on the Court's motion to August 28, 2015. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)
